title: Primary Responsibility to Repatriate Overseas Filipino Seafarers.
content: 26 Notwithstanding the provisions on compulsory insurance coverage as required by law, 27 the repatriation of a seafarer or the latter’s remains, the payment of salaries, benefits, 28 and/or other forms of assistance during detention subject to appropriate guidelines as 29 determined by the DMW, and the transport of personal effects shall be the primary 30 responsibility of the principal/employer and the deploying LMA. This entails the obligation 31 to cover repatriation and attendant costs, including airfare and immigration 32 fines/penalties. This obligation shall be without prior determination of the cause of the 33 need to repatriate the seafarer. After the seafarer has returned to the country, the 34 principal/employer or LMA may, however, recover the cost of repatriation from the 35 seafarer if the termination of the employment was due solely to the seafarer’s fault. 36 37
